description: Interlaced strips, stems, or fibres become 'wiru', a self-supporting container. It usually has an open top and can be carried or used for storage. 'molu' (container) covers the wider category; Household words distinguish bags, boxes, jars, and buckets.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips for voiced 'w', then unround as the tongue rises towards stressed high-front 'i'. Tap the tongue tip briefly at the alveolar ridge for 'r', and finish with high-back rounded 'u'.
sound_symbolism: The movement from narrow front 'i' to rounded back 'u' gives the short word a gathered contour, a useful memory for loose strands drawn into one form.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A basket records its material and method in plain sight. The maker reads the length, bend, and season of each fibre; the finished shape records that attention.

semantic_domains.creation: a container formed through weaving or interlacing
